final_answer:
Use `@blockscout/*` as the published source now, and keep your rebrand via npm aliases instead of raw `@goldscan/*` dependencies.

This matters because `@goldscan/*` is currently not in the registry, so installs/lockfile regeneration will keep failing and your frontend won’t be reproducible.

Next steps:
1. In [scan/goldscan-frontend/package.json](/srv/repos/gold-chain/scan/goldscan-frontend/package.json), replace any `@goldscan/*` deps with alias form like `npm:@blockscout/<pkg>@<version>` (or direct `@blockscout/<pkg>` if you want no alias).
2. Keep code imports on your branded namespace (`@goldchain-scan/*`) only if those aliases exist for every package.
3. Regenerate lockfile (`pnpm install --lockfile-only`), then run `pnpm lint:tsc` and `pnpm build`.
4. If you want true `@goldscan/*`, publish those packages first, then switch dependencies in one pass.
